notes: Token & cost assertions live in basic-turn already; this scenario stresses per-turn accumulation across MULTIPLE turns. The phase DSL only asserts state arcs and event kinds (no TotalTokens form). The token-accounting evidence lives in events.jsonl: each turn boundary emits a PerTurnContribution whose UsageBreakdown carries input/output/cache tokens, and SessionMetrics.TotalTokens accumulates monotonically. After turn 1 TotalTokens > 0; after turn 2 and turn 3 TotalTokens is ≥ the previous total. The recording captures three turns so the monotonic-accumulation invariant is visible across two boundaries (T1→T2 and T2→T3).

text: Turn 1 completes back to ready; first PerTurnContribution flushed on the requestId boundary by turn 2's first event
Turn 2 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T2 ≥ TotalTokens after T1 (monotonic)
Turn 3 completes back to ready; TotalTokens after T3 ≥ TotalTokens after T2 (monotonic accumulation across 3 turns)
